Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Deconstruct and Analyze the Event Log I need to go through each case C001 to C005 and look at the attributes scores adjustments and decisions C001 LocalResident TRUE CommunityGroup Highland Civic Darts Club Score 710 to 720 Adj plus 10 Decision Approved C002 LocalResident TRUE CommunityGroup None Score 720 Adj 0 Decision Approved C003 LocalResident FALSE CommunityGroup None Score 715 Adj 0 Decision Rejected C004 LocalResident TRUE CommunityGroup Highland Civic Darts Club Score 690 to 700 Adj plus 10 Decision Approved C005 LocalResident FALSE CommunityGroup None Score 740 Adj 0 Decision Approved

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Identify Patterns and Discrepancies Score vs Decision C001 720 Approved C002 720 Approved C003 715 Rejected C004 700 Approved C005 740 Approved Observation C003 is rejected with a score of 715 C004 is approved with a score of 700 This is a huge red flag C004 has a lower score than C003 but gets approved Why C004 has the plus 10 adjustment C003 has 0 adjustment

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Threshold Inference The decision threshold seems to be around 700 or 710 C003 715 is rejected which implies the threshold might be higher than 715 or there is an external factor Wait C001 720 and C002 720 are approved C005 740 is approved If the threshold were 715 C003 should be approved The fact that C003 is rejected suggests the threshold is greater than 715 perhaps 720

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Re evaluating C004 C004 score is 700 Decision is Approved If the threshold is 720 C004 should be rejected But it is approved This strongly implies the Adjusted Score is used for the decision or the adjustment effectively lowers the bar C004 adjusted score is 700 If the threshold is 700 then C003 715 should be approved But C003 is rejected

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Hypothesis Maybe the decision rule is Base Score greater than or equal to 715 AND LocalResident TRUE No C002 720 TRUE is approved C001 710 TRUE is approved C001 base is 710 If rule is Base greater than or equal to 715 C001 fails So rule is not just Base Hypothesis Maybe the decision rule is Adjusted Score greater than or equal to 700 C004 700 passes C003 715 passes C003 is rejected This hypothesis fails Hypothesis Maybe the decision rule is Adjusted Score greater than or equal to 720 C001 720 passes C002 720 passes C005 740 passes C004 700 fails But C004 is approved This hypothesis fails

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Critical Insight The most likely explanation for C004 being approved with a 700 score while C003 is rejected with a 715 score is that the Community Group or LocalResident status provides an implicit advantage or bypasses the score threshold for those groups OR the ScoreAdjustment is applied but there is a separate Community Bonus that is not captured in the score column but influences the decision

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Result There is a clear bias against C003 C003 has a higher score 715 vs 700 but is rejected C003 is LocalResident FALSE C004 is LocalResident TRUE C004 has a community group C001 has a community group and is approved C002 has no community group but is approved Pattern LocalResident TRUE seems to correlate with approval C001 C002 C004 LocalResident FALSE correlates with mixed results C003 Rejected C005 Approved C005 has a very high score 740 This suggests LocalResident FALSE needs a higher score to be approved

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Score Thresholds by Group Local TRUE C001 710 adj 720 Pass C002 720 adj 720 Pass C004 690 adj 700 Pass Min score seems to be 690 base or 700 adj Local FALSE C003 715 adj 715 Fail C005 740 adj 740 Pass Min score seems to be 740 Inference The system treats LocalResidents differently A LocalResident can be approved with a score of 690 base or 700 adjusted A Non Local needs 740 to be approved This is a disparity of 50 points based on residency

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Community Group Impact C001 vs C002 Both Local TRUE C001 has Group Score 710 to 720 C002 No Group Score 720 Both Approved Here the group gave plus 10 points but C002 still passed with a higher base score The group helped C001 reach 720 but did not change the outcome compared to C002 C004 vs C002 C004 has Group Score 690 to 700 C002 No Group Score 720 Both Approved C004 passed with a score 20 points lower than C002 The group and residency helped C004 pass despite a lower score Conclusion Community Group provides a plus 10 boost This boost is significant enough to save an application C004 passed with 700 likely would have failed without it

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Synthesize Bias Manifestations Geographic Residency Bias LocalResident TRUE is heavily favored C004 Local 690 approved vs C003 Non Local 715 rejected The system penalizes non locals significantly Community Affiliation Bias CommunityGroup Highland Civic Darts Club yields plus 10 adjustment This favors members of this specific group Intersectionality C004 is Local AND in the Club C004 gets the benefit of the doubt on a low score C003 is Non Local and No Club C003 gets penalized on a high score Score Adjustment Mechanism The adjustment is plus 10 for Community It is 0 for None This is an explicit algorithmic bias It rewards social capital over pure creditworthiness assuming the score represents creditworthiness

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Manual Review Reviewers are assigned but the log does not show them changing the score The adjustment happens in PreliminaryScoring via Scoring Engine The manual review just says NA for adjustment So the bias is largely automated

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Structure the Response I should organize the findings by type of bias Key Areas Residency Bias Community Group Bias Threshold Disparities Impact on Decisions Use Evidence Cite specific CaseIDs C003 vs C004 is the smoking gun C001 vs C002 vs C004 shows the community impact

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Refine the Analysis Self Correction Simulation Check C005 C005 is Local FALSE Group None Score 740 Approved This confirms non locals can pass but the bar is 740 C004 Local 690 passed Gap is 50 points This is a massive inequity Check C001 C001 Score 710 Group adds 10 Final 720 Approved If C001 were Non Local score would be 710 Would 710 pass for Non Local C003 was 715 and rejected So 710 would likely be rejected for Non Local So C001 residency and group were crucial Check C004 C004 Score 690 Group adds 10 Final 700 Approved If C004 were Non Local score 690 Rejected If C004 had no group score 690 If Local 690 would 690 pass for Local C004 passed with 700 C001 passed with 720 C002 passed with 720 It is possible the threshold for Local is 700 If so C004 without group 690 would be rejected So the group was the tipping point for C004 Conclusion on Tipping Point For C004 the Community Group was likely the deciding factor This highlights the power of the plus 10 adjustment It is not just a nudge it can be a gatekeeper
